structural.note: Orchestrator determinism turn count and agent order.

semantic.note: Primary metric meaning equivalence across runs.

lexical_reference.note: Reference baseline only therefore not graded.

requirements.text: The system should allow users to quickly check the availability of meeting rooms and book them instantly without waiting for email confirmations.
The system should send notifications to users when their booking is confirmed or if there are any changes.
The system should allow users to search for rooms based on date, time, and capacity.
The system should provide a fast, self-service experience for booking meeting rooms.
The system should allow users to find and reserve rooms that meet their needs quickly.
The system should include oversight features to prevent misuse of room bookings.
The system should allow users to book rooms based on predefined criteria, such as room capacity relative to the number of attendees.
The system should provide the office manager with the ability to set limits on room bookings, such as restricting large rooms for small meetings.
The system must prevent double bookings of the same room at the same time to avoid conflicts.
The system should filter rooms based on capacity, equipment, and location to match meeting requirements.
The system should allow users to filter rooms based on required technology or amenities to meet specific needs.
The system should send booking confirmation and reminder notifications to users to ensure awareness of meeting details and reduce no-shows.
The system should provide filtering options for meeting rooms that include essential technology and amenities.
The system must allow users to modify or cancel their bookings easily to accommodate last-minute changes.
The system must allow users to cancel their bookings with a simple click, ideally with a confirmation prompt to prevent accidental cancellations.
The system must automatically release rooms if a meeting has not started within 10 minutes of the scheduled time to improve room utilization.
The system should allow users to quickly check the availability of meeting rooms and book them instantly without waiting for email confirmations.
The system should send notifications to users when their booking is confirmed or if there are any changes.
The system should allow users to search for rooms based on date, time, and capacity.
The system should help users secure meeting rooms immediately when needed, especially during tight schedules.
The system must prevent double booking of the same room at the same time to avoid conflicts.
The system must implement real-time availability checks to ensure accurate booking status and prevent double bookings.
The system should lock the room for a brief period during the booking process to avoid conflicts.
The system must allow users to modify or cancel their bookings easily to accommodate changing plans.
The system must update the reservation in real-time when a booking is modified.
The system must immediately free up the room when a booking is canceled.
The system must automatically release a room if a meeting has not started within 10 minutes of the scheduled time.
The system should provide a user-friendly interface for modifying or canceling bookings, including clear buttons, a simple calendar view, and a list of current bookings.
The system should allow users to filter available rooms based on specific amenities to better meet their needs.
The system should allow users to filter available rooms based on essential amenities such as video conferencing capabilities, whiteboards, and projectors to streamline the booking process.
The system must send booking confirmation and reminder notifications to users to keep them informed about their reservations and any changes.
The system should allow users to quickly check the availability of meeting rooms and book them instantly without waiting for email confirmations.
The system should send notifications to users when their booking is confirmed or if there are any changes.
The system should allow users to search for rooms based on date, time, and capacity.
The system should prevent double booking of the same room at the same time to avoid conflicts and ensure reliability of reservations.
The system should provide a fast, self-service experience to enhance productivity and minimize administrative tasks.
The system should allow users to select rooms based on their capacity and provide warnings or restrictions for bookings that exceed the required capacity.
The system should allow the office manager to set rules for room bookings, including limiting the maximum capacity for certain rooms based on their intended use.
The system should allow the office manager to set maximum capacity limits for each room based on its intended use to ensure appropriate bookings.
The system should enable the office manager to restrict bookings for certain rooms during peak hours or for specific types of meetings to manage resource availability effectively.
The system should provide analytics on room usage patterns to help the office manager adjust booking rules based on actual utilization data.
The system should provide insights on room usage patterns, including frequency of bookings, peak usage times, and average number of attendees, to assist the office manager in decision-making.
The system should track cancellations and no-shows to provide data for understanding room utilization and adjusting booking rules.
The system must capture specific data points related to bookings, including date, time, duration, and meeting occurrence.
The system must track the frequency of no-shows for each room to inform booking rule adjustments.
The system should automatically release rooms if a meeting has not started within 10 minutes of the scheduled time.
The system must provide a quick and self-service way to check room availability and book instantly without waiting for email confirmations.
The system must allow users to modify or cancel reservations easily.
The system must send notifications when a booking is confirmed or if there are any changes.
The system must prevent double bookings to avoid conflicts when users arrive at the room.
The system must allow users to easily change the date, time, or capacity of their bookings.
The system must provide visibility of upcoming bookings for a room to help users make informed decisions.
The system must provide a streamlined process for canceling a booking without multiple steps or waiting for email confirmations.
The system must provide immediate feedback on the cancellation status to inform users if it was successful.
The system must provide booking details, such as the date, time, and room that was canceled, in the cancellation feedback.
The system must provide a confirmation message indicating that the room is now available for others to book after a cancellation.
The system must provide a clear error message if the cancellation fails for any reason.
The system must provide a separate view for upcoming bookings to allow users to assess room availability without interrupting other processes.
The system must allow users to filter upcoming bookings by date and time to quickly find relevant information.
The system must allow users to set reminders for their own bookings to ensure they do not forget them.
The system must allow users to filter upcoming bookings by room type to meet specific meeting requirements.
The system must allow users to filter upcoming bookings by capacity to ensure the selected room can accommodate all participants.
The system should allow users to quickly check the availability of meeting rooms and book them instantly without waiting for email confirmations.
The system should send notifications to users when their booking is confirmed or if there are any changes.
The system should allow users to search for rooms based on date, time, and capacity.
The system should allow users to specify the date and time of the meeting when searching for rooms.
The system should allow users to filter meeting rooms based on their capacity.
The system should allow users to filter rooms by features such as video conferencing capabilities or whiteboards.
The system should provide real-time availability of meeting rooms to facilitate quick decision-making.
The system should provide visual indicators, such as color coding, to quickly assess room availability.
The system should send alerts for when a room becomes available.
The system must prevent double booking of the same room at the same time.
The system should provide users with a clear notification when a room is unavailable, including details of the existing booking.
The system should suggest alternative available rooms when a user attempts to book a room that is already reserved.
The system should provide users with the ability to modify or cancel their bookings easily.
The system should allow users to modify the date, time, or capacity of their bookings and also provide an option to cancel bookings entirely.
The system should send confirmation notifications to users whenever they modify or cancel a booking.

The system should send confirmation notifications that include specific details about modified bookings, such as updated date, time, and capacity, as well as summaries of cancellations including original booking details.
The system should provide a way for users to view their upcoming bookings in a consolidated list to manage their schedule effectively and avoid potential conflicts.

issues.description: Jamie wants a fast, self-service experience while also recognizing the need for oversight to prevent misuse.
Jamie wants a fast, self-service experience while also recognizing the need for oversight to prevent misuse.
The system must provide a straightforward interface for users to access their reservations quickly.
The system must allow users to modify the date, time, or room of their bookings.
The system must allow users to cancel their bookings with a simple click, ideally with a confirmation prompt.
The system must automatically release rooms if a meeting has not started within 10 minutes of the scheduled time.
Jamie wants a fast, self-service experience while Sarah prioritizes oversight to prevent misuse of resources.
Currently, there is a problem with employees booking large rooms for small meetings, leading to resource misuse.
The system must log the reason for cancellations if provided by the user to identify patterns or issues.
The system must track the frequency of no-shows for each room to inform decisions about booking policies.
The system should automatically release rooms if a meeting has not started within 10 minutes of the scheduled time.
The need for a quick cancellation process conflicts with the office manager's need for oversight and control over bookings.
The ability to cancel a booking quickly with minimal clicks.
The system must provide visibility of upcoming bookings for a room to help users make informed decisions.
The current process for canceling a booking is lengthy and involves multiple steps, causing frustration for users.
Users currently do not receive immediate feedback on the cancellation status, leading to uncertainty about whether the cancellation was successful.
The ability to view upcoming bookings in a separate view.
The ability to filter upcoming bookings by date and time.
The ability to set reminders for personal bookings.
The ability to filter upcoming bookings by capacity.
Users currently do not receive clear notifications when a room is unavailable, leading to confusion about existing bookings.
Users currently lack the ability to easily modify or cancel their bookings, which contributes to no-shows and poor room utilization.

gt_coverage.note: Primary score = SBERT >= 0.45 coverage. Coverage at 0.60 and 0.70 reported for sensitivity analysis. TF-IDF reported as secondary reference only.

per_gt.ground_truth: Users must be able to search for available meeting rooms by date, time, and capacity.
Users must be able to book, modify, and cancel room reservations.
The system must send booking confirmation and reminder notifications to the user.
The office manager must be able to view all bookings across all rooms.
The system must prevent double booking of the same room at the same time.
The system must be accessible via web and mobile.

req_reproducibility.interpretation: Measures whether the same requirements were elicited across runs, regardless of wording.
